reasoning: The question resolves to whether the latest GASDESW (US diesel price) value on 2026-04-05 is higher than its value on 2026-03-29. Since GASDESW updates on Mondays, this means comparing the March 30, 2026 release to the March 23, 2026 release ($5.375). Retail diesel prices closely track benchmark crude. Over the week of March 23-27, oil markets experienced significant volatility due to an ongoing conflict in the Middle East and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. Although prices dipped slightly early in the week on hopes of peace talks, they surged again late in the week, with Brent crude hitting $111 on Friday, March 27 (a 5.7% daily jump from Thursday). Additionally, an alternative source (AAA) observed diesel prices ticking up to ~$5.38 by Thursday, March 26. Because retail prices ("pump prices") respond very quickly to wholesale increases, the late-week surge in crude oil makes it highly probable that the EIA survey taken on Monday, March 30 will report an average price higher than $5.375. While minor mean-reverting effects or last-minute government SPR releases could dampen the spike, the strong momentum of late-week crude prices heavily favors an increase. Estimated probability: 77%.
